questiontext.text:
Give a polynomial (of {@x@} and nothing else) to plot.

[[input:ans1]] [[validation:ans1]]

questionvariables.text: /* No wars we are just plotting the student input. */

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.

truefeedback.text:
Your polynomial as plotted by string replacement, won't survive if you wrap that polynomial in sqrt(...):

[[jsxgraph]] [[comment]] Use the [[jsstring]]-block instead of "{#...#}" if there is any risk that the value has something that would need escaping. If it is student input then there most definitely is that risk. [[/comment]] var ex = [[jsstring]]{#ans1#}[[/jsstring]]; ex = ex.replaceAll("^", "**"); var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, {boundingbox: [-10, 5, 10, -5], axis: true}); [[comment]] This is not the way you want to do this, if there is any chance that the student can push arbitrary strings through the validation. [[/comment]] var f = (x) => eval(ex); board.create('functiongraph', [f]); [[/jsxgraph]]

Your polynomial as plotted by direct JessieCode

[[jsxgraph]] [[comment]] Use the [[jsstring]]-block instead of "{#...#}" if there is any risk that the value has something that would need escaping. If it is student input then there most definitely is that risk. [[/comment]] var ex = [[jsstring]]{#ans1#}[[/jsstring]]; var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, {boundingbox: [-10, 5, 10, -5], axis: true}); [[comment]] This is safer as we parse the thing to JessieCode which is safe to evaluate [[/comment]] var f = board.jc.snippet(ex, true, 'x', true); board.create('functiongraph', [f]); [[/jsxgraph]]

Finally, a numerical plot for cases where it just is no polynomial.

[[jsxgraph]] [[comment]] Note that sane authors don't write this type of code inline, but one might use the include logic to inline it anyway. Start by generating the points to evaluate. Use the second derivative as the adaptive metric for dropping extraneous points. We do the evaluation using a [[define/]] [[/comment]] [[define _minx="-10" _maxx="10" _minstep="(_maxx-_minx)/500" _maxstep="(_maxx-_minx)/10" _d2="diff(ans1,x,2)" _x="makelist(ev(_x,numer),_x,_minx,_maxx,_minstep)" _y="makelist(ev(ans1,x=__x,numer),__x,_x)" /]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, {boundingbox: [-10, 5, 10, -5], axis: true}); board.create('curve', [{#_x#},{#_y#}]); [[/jsxgraph]]
